saints.name: San Vito
saints.description: Fue un niño mártir siciliano, venerado como patrón de bailarines, actores y epilépticos, y forma parte de los Catorce Santos Auxiliares.

saints.name: San Benito de Aniane
saints.description: Un reformador monástico influyente durante el reinado de Carlomagno, conocido por promover la Regla de San Benito y unificar la vida monástica en el Imperio Carolingio.

observances.name: Día Mundial de Toma de Conciencia del Abuso y Maltrato en la Vejez
observances.description: Establecido por la ONU, busca generar conciencia sobre las diversas formas de abuso y maltrato que sufren las personas mayores, promoviendo su dignidad y derechos.

observances.name: Día Mundial del Viento
observances.description: Una iniciativa para promover la energía eólica como fuente de energía limpia y renovable, educando sobre su potencial y beneficios para un futuro sostenible.

historicalQuote.text: Ningún hombre libre será arrestado o encarcelado o privado de sus derechos o de sus bienes, ni declarado fuera de la ley ni exiliado ni molestado de ninguna forma, salvo por el juicio legal de sus iguales o por la ley del país.
historicalQuote.author: Carta Magna
historicalQuote.context: Este texto fundamental de la libertad es una de las cláusulas más célebres de la Carta Magna, un documento que el Rey Juan de Inglaterra selló el 15 de junio de 1215. Estableció principios del debido proceso y limitó el poder monárquico, sentando las bases de la democracia constitucional y el estado de derecho.

births.name: Erik Erikson
births.description: Psicoanalista germano-estadounidense, conocido por su influyente teoría del desarrollo psicosocial y el concepto de la 'crisis de identidad', que ha enriquecido profundamente la psicología del desarrollo.

births.name: Mario Cuomo
births.description: Político estadounidense y exgobernador de Nueva York, reconocido por su elocuencia, sus políticas progresistas y su destacada carrera pública que marcó una era en la política de su estado.

events.title: Se sella la Carta Magna
events.description: El Rey Juan de Inglaterra sella la Carta Magna en Runnymede, un documento histórico que estableció límites al poder real y garantizó derechos fundamentales, convirtiéndose en un pilar de las libertades constitucionales.

events.title: Primer vuelo transatlántico sin escalas
events.description: Los aviadores británicos John Alcock y Arthur Brown completan con éxito el primer vuelo transatlántico sin escalas a bordo de un Vickers Vimy, marcando un hito extraordinario en la historia de la aviación.

events.title: Charles Goodyear patenta el caucho vulcanizado
events.description: El inventor estadounidense Charles Goodyear recibe una patente por su revolucionario proceso de vulcanización del caucho, una innovación que transformó la industria y la fabricación de innumerables productos.

saints.name: St. Vitus
saints.description: An early Christian martyr, often invoked as one of the Fourteen Holy Helpers, St. Vitus is traditionally associated with healing and is the patron saint of dancers and those suffering from epilepsy.

saints.name: St. Germaine Cousin
saints.description: A French peasant girl (1579-1601) who, despite being born with deformities and suffering neglect and abuse, lived a life of deep faith and charity, becoming a patroness of abandoned people and the disabled.

observances.name: World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
observances.description: Recognized by the United Nations, this day aims to raise global awareness of the cultural, social, economic, and demographic processes affecting elder abuse and neglect.

observances.name: Global Wind Day
observances.description: An international day for discovering wind energy, promoting its power and the ways it can reshape energy systems, celebrated through events worldwide.

historicalQuote.text: No free man shall be seized or imprisoned, or stripped of his rights or possessions, or outlawed or exiled, or deprived of his standing in any other way, nor will we proceed with force against him, or send others to do so, except by the lawful judgment of his equals or by the law of the land.
historicalQuote.author: The Magna Carta (King John I)
historicalQuote.context: This powerful statement is a clause from the Magna Carta, a historic document sealed by King John I of England on June 15, 1215. It established fundamental principles of individual rights and limited monarchical power, serving as a cornerstone for constitutional law worldwide.

births.name: Edvard Grieg
births.description: A celebrated Norwegian composer and pianist, Grieg (1843-1907) is best known for his Piano Concerto in A minor, the Peer Gynt incidental music, and his lyrical piano pieces that embody Norwegian folk traditions.

births.name: Helen Hunt
births.description: An acclaimed American actress, director, and screenwriter, Hunt (born 1963) has won multiple awards, including an Academy Award for Best Actress for 'As Good as It Gets' and four Emmy Awards for her role in 'Mad About You'.

births.name: Ice Cube
births.description: An influential American rapper, actor, and filmmaker, Ice Cube (born O'Shea Jackson Sr., 1969) rose to fame as a member of N.W.A, pioneered gangsta rap, and has built a successful career in film, starring in and producing numerous hit movies.

events.title: Magna Carta Sealed
events.description: On June 15, 1215, King John of England sealed the Magna Carta, a foundational document that asserted legal rights and limited the power of the monarch, inspiring constitutional law and human rights movements globally.

events.title: First Non-stop Transatlantic Flight Completed
events.description: In a remarkable feat of aviation, John Alcock and Arthur Brown completed the first non-stop transatlantic flight on June 15, 1919, flying from Newfoundland to Ireland and demonstrating the boundless potential of air travel.

events.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour's Maiden Voyage
events.description: The Space Shuttle Endeavour successfully completed its maiden flight, STS-49, on June 15, 1992, marking a significant milestone in space exploration and demonstrating advanced capabilities for repairing satellites in orbit.
